Motion For Protective Order And To Compel Re Deposition Of Person Most Qualified And Request For Production Of Documents To Defendant Amazon Logistics, Inc.
Off Calendar Jul-27-2023 Off Calendar

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-24-2023 Off Calendar

Motion To Compel Deposition Testimony Of Witness Kyle Andeer
Off Calendar Jul-13-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference
Krista Stoumbos, Esq., Wilson, Elser, Moskowitz, Edelman & Dicker, 415-433-0990 for Defendant Amazon Logistics, Inc.
No other appearances.

Plaintiff Counsel emailed the Court to state that proper notice was not provided on this mnotion and that plaintiff's would not be appearing on this matter. (7/12/23 email forwarded to JPT William Lynn)

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT AMAZON LOGISTICS, INC.'S Motion To Compel Deposition Testimony Of Witness Kyle Andeer is denied for failure to comply with CCP 1005.

Motion denied as untimely since deadlines for discovery and motions concerning discovery passed long ago as this matter was already assigned to a trial judge in 2022, and there is no pending motion by any party to reopen / extend discovery motion deadline. Pelton-Shepherd Industries, Inc. v. Delta Packaging Products, Inc., 165 Cal. App. 4th 1568 (2008).

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
